answer:
Let the angles of the pentagon be $3x, 3x, 3x, 4x, 5x$. The sum of the angles in a pentagon is $(5-2) \times 180^\circ = 3 \times 180^\circ = 540^\circ$.
So, $3x + 3x + 3x + 4x + 5x = 540^\circ$.
$18x = 540^\circ$.
$x = \frac{540^\circ}{18} = 30^\circ$.
The angles are $3x = 3(30^\circ) = 90^\circ$, $3x = 3(30^\circ) = 90^\circ$, $3x = 3(30^\circ) = 90^\circ$, $4x = 4(30^\circ) = 120^\circ$, and $5x = 5(30^\circ) = 150^\circ$.
The largest angle is $5x = 150^\circ$.

```python
print(150)
```
